_note: Three primary dimensioning methods: chained (running dimensions from common datum), parallel (each from a common datum), and running (cumulative from datum). Leaders connect dimension text to features; arrowheads indicate dimension line termination.

chained.description: Sequential dimensions, each starting where the previous ended
chained.use: Long objects with similar features at regular intervals; tolerances accumulate

parallel.description: Each dimension from a common datum (origin)
parallel.use: When tolerance from one datum is critical; tolerances do NOT accumulate

running.description: Cumulative dimensions from a single datum on one line
running.use: Compact representation when many dimensions reference one datum
running.tolerance_behavior: Tolerances are independent (compact form of parallel)

leaders_and_arrowheads.leader_line: Solid thin line connecting dimension text to feature
leaders_and_arrowheads.arrowhead_form: Filled triangle (15° to 30° included angle) terminating dimension line at the feature edge
leaders_and_arrowheads.extension_lines: Solid thin lines extending from feature edges to dimension line, with small gap from feature

engine_lookup._purpose: Flat key/value form for runtime engine consumption
